title: AI platform observability baseline
content: Platform AI services should emit structured logs, correlation identifiers, provider metadata, latency measurements, and clear error categories. Traceable requests are essential for production support and auditability.

title: Enterprise guardrails policy
content: Guardrails should validate inputs before model invocation, detect prompt attacks, block secret exposure, and produce explainable policy decisions. Safe rejection paths are part of platform reliability, not only security.

title: Retrieval pattern for enterprise knowledge
content: Retrieval improves enterprise answers by grounding prompts in approved documents such as runbooks, standards, and architecture notes. Retrieval should be observable and should expose the chosen sources for review.

title: Model provider abstraction
content: Platform services should isolate provider-specific logic behind a stable interface so teams can switch between mock, managed cloud inference, or future providers without rewriting orchestration and policy layers.

title: Evaluation driven platform quality
content: AI platforms should evaluate prompt and response quality using repeatable test cases before releasing changes. Small built-in evals are useful even when full model benchmarking is not yet available.
